page: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-480s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It is powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, with 2 physical cores operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU features a shared allocation model and includes 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. Storage is bundled with a 480 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a mixed performance profile. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is in the top tier, and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression are strong. However, multi-core CPU performance and large-block memory bandwidth fall into the average tier. The server exhibits weaknesses in other areas, with passmark CPU scores, database operations, and Redis SET operations scoring in the bottom 25%. Static web serving throughput is poor, placing in the bottom 10%, and LLM inference for a medium 7B model is also poor at 4.99 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU allocation lowers the resource cost relative to the generous 16.0 GB memory capacity and 480 GB NVMe storage. However, the shared CPU model introduces potential performance variability, making it less suitable for sustained high-compute workloads. This server is best aligned with memory-intensive but CPU-burstable workloads, such as caching layers, lightweight data processing, or development environments that benefit from a high memory-to-vCPU ratio without requiring dedicated CPU execution.
description: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-480s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and 480 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this instance offers a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Benchmark results show top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain average. Conversely, database operations, Redis SET throughput, and static web serving fall into the lower tiers. The shared CPU allocation provides a cost-effective option for memory-heavy workloads, though it introduces resource-sharing tradeoffs. This server is suited for memory-intensive, bursty applications, development environments, and lightweight data processing tasks that do not require dedicated CPU cores.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-480s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server with 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and a 480 GB NVMe SSD, offering strong single-core but weaker database performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-480s-amd features 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and 480 GB NVMe storage for memory-intensive, bursty workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered virtual server combining a high memory-to-core ratio and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive, burstable workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC Genoa processor with 2 shared vCPUs and x86_64 architecture
16.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
Bundled 480 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance but weaker database and web serving throughput
Cost-effective shared CPU model suitable for memory-heavy, bursty workloads
